rule: Open with a what-if, contrarian, or FOMO hook — never a hashtag or news announcement
reason: What-if hooks (Code to Figma: 22.4K), identity hooks (India's answer: 19K), and FOMO hooks (Claude courses: save magnet) all outperform news openers (UPI: 5.2K). FOMO angle added from Anthropic reel.

rule: Target 31–36 seconds. Never go over 38.
reason: JioFrames at 41s underperformed vs potential. Indus at 38s got consistent push but lower peak. Sub-34s reels (Supabase 32s, Claude 33s) are the new target.

rule: Chase saves and shares, not likes. Saves = algorithm fuel.
reason: Code to Figma: 435 saves, 406 shares = 22.4K views. Indus: 10 saves, 10 shares = steady curve. Saves:views ratio is the best signal. Claude reel built explicitly as a save magnet.

rule: Use the India vs West frame whenever possible
reason: Top 3 organic reels use this angle. Indus AI (1,999 views), India's Meta glasses (19K), JioFrames (2,954) all India-framed. Even Supabase block is an India story.

rule: Lead with a strong product or screen visual — no plain logo graphics
reason: UPI reel used logo only = 5.2K. All stronger reels had product shots, screen recordings, or app UI. B-roll quality directly impacts retention.

rule: Use triple short sentences for rhythm at least once per script
reason: Creates punch and makes it feel spoken, not written. e.g. 'No studio. No photographer. No budget.' or 'No warning. No reason. Just gone.'

rule: End utility reels with a direct save CTA. End opinion reels with a debate-inviting question.
reason: Utility reels (Claude courses, Supabase alternatives) need 'save this' at the end. Opinion/identity reels (Indus, JioFrames) need an open question. Match CTA to content type.

rule: Avoid fintech, infra, and policy topics unless tied to a design or product angle
reason: UPI for Agents (5.2K) = niche infra. Supabase block works because it's a developer tool story, not a policy story. Always find the product/people angle.

rule: No intro. Zero. Straight into the hook from frame 1.
reason: Every top reel opens cold. Any warm-up kills retention in the first 2 seconds.

rule: Drop one specific technical detail or number per script for credibility
reason: '105 billion parameter model', '₹10,000', 'Section 69A', 'zero rupees' — specifics signal authority and stop vague generalism.

rule: Prioritize AI design tools, AI hardware, India tech narratives, and utility resource reels
reason: Top reels all sit in these categories. Utility resource reels (Claude courses, Supabase alternatives) are now confirmed as a fourth strong lane — save magnets.

rule: Rate every topic before scripting — only write scripts for topics scoring 7+/10
reason: Not every tweet is reel-worthy. Rating filters effort and keeps quality high.

rule: Use punchy, opinionated language — never soft or hedged
reason: 'India can't touch it without blocking Google' outperforms 'may be harder to block'. Rahul's audience responds to confidence, not caveats.

rule: Script first, audio second, video third — never skip approval gates
reason: Each phase costs time and money. Catching issues at script stage is cheapest. Proven after multiple iterations on Indus + Supabase reels.

rule: Always pull source context via X API before writing — never script from memory
reason: Fresh data = accurate scripts = credibility. Also reveals engagement signals (6.9M impressions on Claude tweet = topic has legs).

rule: B-roll links must be attached before sending to Peeyush — include edit flow notes
reason: Editor needs visual references AND timing notes to cut efficiently. No b-roll = slower edit = delayed post.

rule: Target >1.5% share rate and >0.5% save rate
reason: Code to Figma: 1.8% shares, 1.9% saves. Google 3D print: 3.3% shares. Indus: 0.5% shares/saves. Low share rate = algorithm won't push. Saves matter more than likes.

rule: Ride trending topics within 24–48h of breaking — speed is distribution
reason: Claude courses tweet was 6.9M impressions day 1. Supabase block was 2 days old. Both had runway. Day 5+ is too late.

rule: Steady curve > spike-then-plateau. Aim for consistent algorithm push over 24h+
reason: JioFrames spiked at 12h then died (2,954 total). Indus grew steadily and tracked WITH typical reel curve (1,999, still moving). Retention quality beats early spike.

rule: Caption style matters — log which caption type was used for every reel
reason: Started tracking Mar 2 (Claude reel: FOMO Option A). Need 5+ data points to know if FOMO vs contrarian vs punchy captions drive more saves. Treat captions as a variable.

rule: Don't become a 'save for later' account — balance content types
reason: 'Companies paying lakhs' got 249 saves but only 10 profile visits. High saves = people hoarding, not converting to followers. Mix bookmark reels with useful/entertaining ones that keep active viewers. Target: ~40% useful, ~30% news, ~20% entertainment, ~10% bookmark.

rule: High saves ≠ high growth. Watch profile_activity as the growth signal.
reason: Code to Figma: 159 profile visits (real growth). Companies paying lakhs: 10 profile visits despite 249 saves. Saves fuel algorithm reach but profile_activity converts to followers. Need both.
